CURRENT STATE: Line 84 uses bare 525_960 with comment "~1 year in minutes". Line 88 error message mentions "525960 minutes / ~1 year".

CHANGES:
- Add: pub const MAX_ESTIMATED_MINUTES: i32 = 525_960; // ~1 year in minutes (365.25 * 24 * 60)
- Line 84: Replace > 525_960 with > MAX_ESTIMATED_MINUTES
- Line 88: Replace hardcoded "525960" in error message with format using MAX_ESTIMATED_MINUTES

NOTE: Use i32 (not usize) because estimated_minutes is Option<i32> in the Issue struct.  {"id":"beads_rust-cyg8","title":"Integrate rich output into list command","description":"## Command: br list

### Traffic Level: HIGHEST
This is the most frequently used command. Every agent session, every human check starts here. The visual transformation here has the biggest impact.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/list.rs
Output: Plain text table via format strings

### Integration Steps
1. Import OutputContext and IssueTable from output module
2. Accept &OutputContext in execute() signature
3. Replace manual table formatting with IssueTable::new()
4. Configure columns based on --fields argument
5. Handle --json mode (return early with serde_json output)
6. Handle --quiet mode (IDs only)

### Code Changes
```rust
// Before
pub fn execute(args: &ListArgs, json: bool, overrides: &CliOverrides) -> Result<()> {
    // ... fetch issues ...
    for issue in &issues {
        println!("{}", format_issue_row(issue));
    }
}

// After  
pub fn execute(args: &ListArgs, ctx: &OutputContext, overrides: &CliOverrides) -> Result<()> {
    // ... fetch issues ...
    
    IssueTable::new(&issues, ctx)
        .columns(&args.fields.as_deref().unwrap_or(&["id", "status", "title"]))
        .highlight_ready(true)
        .render()
}
```

### Column Configuration
Default columns: id, status, priority, title
With --all: id, status, priority, type, title, assignee, labels, updated
Custom: respect --fields argument

### Backward Compatibility
- --json produces identical output structure
- --robot is alias for --json (agent mode)
- Exit codes unchanged
- Sort order unchanged

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/list_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_uses_issue_table_component() {
    // Verify list command uses IssueTable for rendering
    let issues = create_test_issues(5);
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let table = IssueTable::new(&issues, &ctx);
    let output = table.render();
    for issue in &issues {
        assert!(output.contains(&issue.id));
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_list_json_output_byte_identical() {
    // Critical: JSON output must not change at all
    let current = run_list_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/list.json");
    assert_eq!(current, baseline, "JSON output must be byte-identical");
}

#[test]
fn test_list_respects_column_config() {
    // --fields should control which columns appear
    let issues = create_test_issues(3);
    let output = run_list_with_fields(&["id", "title"]);
    assert!(output.contains("title"));
    assert!(!output.contains("assignee")); // Not in fields
}

#[test]
fn test_list_quiet_mode_outputs_ids_only() {
    let issues = create_test_issues(3);
    let ctx = OutputContext::quiet();
    let output = render_list(&issues, &ctx);
    // Should be just IDs, one per line
    let lines: Vec<_> = output.lines().collect();
    assert_eq!(lines.len(), 3);
    assert!(lines.iter().all(|l| l.starts_with("beads_rust-")));
}

#[test]
fn test_list_sort_order_maintained() {
    // Priority desc, then created asc
    let issues = create_issues_with_varying_priority();
    let output = render_list(&issues, &OutputContext::plain());
    // Verify P0 before P1 before P2
}

#[test]
fn test_list_empty_results() {
    let issues: Vec<Issue> = vec![];
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_list(&issues, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("No issues") || output.is_empty());
}
```

Location: tests/integration/list_integration.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_command_basic() {
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["list"])
        .output();
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_list_with_filters() {
    // Test --status, --priority, --type, --label filters
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["list", "--status", "open", "--priority", "0,1"])
        .output();
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_list_json_vs_baseline() {
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["list", "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let current: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&result.stdout).unwrap();
    let baseline: serde_json::Value = 
        serde_json::from_str(include_str!("../../fixtures/json_baseline/list.json")).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(current, baseline);
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/list_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: List Command ==="

# Setup
setup_test_db
create_diverse_test_issues # Different statuses, priorities, types

# Test 1: Basic list
log_step "Testing basic list command"
OUTPUT=$(br list 2>&1)
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "beads_rust-" "Should show issue IDs"
log_pass "Basic list works"

# Test 2: JSON backward compatibility (CRITICAL)
log_step "Testing JSON backward compatibility"
CURRENT_JSON=$(br list --json)
BASELINE_JSON=$(cat tests/fixtures/json_baseline/list.json)
# Normalize and compare
CURRENT_NORM=$(echo "$CURRENT_JSON" | jq -S .)
BASELINE_NORM=$(echo "$BASELINE_JSON" | jq -S .)
if [ "$CURRENT_NORM" != "$BASELINE_NORM" ]; then
    log_fail "JSON output differs from baseline!"
    diff <(echo "$BASELINE_NORM") <(echo "$CURRENT_NORM") || true
    exit 1
fi
log_pass "JSON backward compatibility verified"

# Test 3: Status filter
log_step "Testing status filter"
OPEN_OUTPUT=$(br list --status open --json | jq -r '.[].status')
assert_all_equal "$OPEN_OUTPUT" "open" "All issues should be open"
log_pass "Status filter works"

# Test 4: Priority filter
log_step "Testing priority filter"
P0_OUTPUT=$(br list --priority 0 --json | jq -r '.[].priority')
assert_all_equal "$P0_OUTPUT" "0" "All issues should be P0"
log_pass "Priority filter works"

# Test 5: Quiet mode (IDs only)
log_step "Testing quiet mode"
QUIET_OUTPUT=$(br list --quiet 2>&1)
# Each line should be just an ID
assert_lines_match "$QUIET_OUTPUT" "^beads_rust-[a-z0-9]+$" "Each line should be an ID"
log_pass "Quiet mode works"

# Test 6: Column configuration
log_step "Testing column configuration"
FIELDS_OUTPUT=$(br list --fields id,title 2>&1)
# Should NOT contain priority column header in plain mode
log_pass "Column config works"

# Test 7: Rich mode table rendering
log_step "Testing rich mode table"
RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br list 2>&1 || true)
assert_not_empty "$RICH_OUTPUT" "Rich mode should produce output"
log_pass "Rich mode table renders"

# Test 8: Empty results
log_step "Testing empty results"
EMPTY_OUTPUT=$(br list --status tombstone 2>&1 || true)
# Should handle gracefully
log_pass "Empty results handled"

log_success "=== List command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log when list command starts with filter parameters
- Log number of issues matching filters
- Log rendering mode selected (rich/plain/json/quiet)
- Log column configuration being used
- Log completion time for performance monitoring

Scope:
- Mutate command-level sync scenarios in isolated temp workspaces: DB state, JSONL state, base snapshot, tombstones, sidecar files, config paths, BEADS_JSONL-like overrides, and mode flags for import/flush/merge/status/rebuild where appropriate.
- Exercise path validation, atomic write/export, import, merge, rebuild, and recovery boundaries without touching source files or the real repository .beads directory.
- Assert no panic, no hang, no writes outside the allowed temp .beads tree, and structured errors for invalid combinations.
- Check post-run invariants: DB can reopen, JSONL remains parseable when command reports success, recovery artifacts stay under .beads/.br_recovery, dirty flags/base snapshots are coherent, and parent directories are not escaped through traversal or symlink tricks.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo-fuzz/libFuzzer target or equivalent coverage-guided harness exists for sync-path state machines.
- Harness records filesystem writes and rejects writes outside the isolated workspace.
- Seed corpus includes corrupted DB families, stale WAL/SHM sidecars, missing base snapshots, symlinked .beads, custom JSONL paths, and force flag combinations.

 {"id":"beads_rust-d09","title":"label Command Group Implementation","description":"# label Command Group

## Purpose
Implement classic label management with correct JSON shapes, reserved label handling, and idempotent operations.

## CLI
```
br label add <issue...> <label>
br label remove <issue...> <label>
br label list [issue]
br label list-all
br label rename <old-name> <new-name>
```

## Flags
- `<issue...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution supported).
- `<label>`: Label name (case-sensitive, alphanumeric + dash + underscore).
- `--json`: Output as JSON.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output.

## Behavior

### label add
1. Resolve issue ID(s) via partial matching.
2. For each issue:
   - Check if label already exists (idempotent - no error if exists).
   - Validate label format (alphanumeric, dash, underscore).
   - Check for reserved prefix `provides:` - warn/reject.
   - Add label to issue.
   - Mark issue as dirty.
   - Emit `label_added` event.
3. Return results.

### label remove
1. Resolve issue ID(s).
2. For each issue:
   - Check if label exists (idempotent - no error if missing).
   - Remove label.
   - Mark issue as dirty.
   - Emit `label_removed` event.
3. Return results.

### label list [issue]
- If issue provided: return labels for that issue.
- If no issue: return all unique labels in the project.

### label list-all
Return all labels with issue counts:
```json
[
  {"label": "bug", "count": 15},
  {"label": "feature", "count": 8}
]
```

### label rename
1. Find all issues with old label.
2. For each: remove old, add new.
3. Atomic operation.

## Reserved Labels
- `provides:*` prefix is reserved for capability tracking.
- In br, reject with error or warn (configurable).

## Output

### JSON (add/remove)
```json
[
  {"status": "added", "issue_id": "bd-abc12", "label": "urgent"},
  {"status": "exists", "issue_id": "bd-def34", "label": "urgent"}
]
```

### JSON (list for issue)
```json
["bug", "priority-high", "backend"]
```

### JSON (list-all)
```json
[
  {"label": "bug", "count": 15},
  {"label": "feature", "count": 8},
  {"label": "urgent", "count": 3}
]
```

### Text Output (add)
```
✓ Added label urgent to bd-abc12
✓ Label urgent already exists on bd-def34
```

### Text Output (list-all)
```
Labels (3 total):
  bug (15 issues)
  feature (8 issues)
  urgent (3 issues)
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: If ID doesnt resolve → error with suggestions.
- **InvalidLabel**: If label contains invalid characters → error.
- **ReservedLabel**: If label starts with reserved prefix → error/warn.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(issue_id = %id, label = %label, "Adding label");
tracing::debug!(already_exists = exists, "Label status check");
tracing::info!(issue_id = %id, label = %label, "Label added");
tracing::warn!(label = %label, "Attempted to add reserved label");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shapes match bd.
- Label casing preserved (case-sensitive).
- Add/remove are idempotent (no error if exists/missing).
- Reserved label handling matches bd behavior.

## Unit Tests (storage layer)
```rust

test_add_label_idempotent
test_add_label_marks_dirty
test_add_label_writes_event

test_remove_label_basic

test_remove_label_marks_dirty
test_remove_label_writes_event
test_list_labels_for_issue
test_list_labels_empty
test_list_all_labels
test_list_all_with_counts
test_label_case_sensitive
test_label_validation_alphanumeric
test_label_validation_dash_underscore
test_label_validation_invalid_chars_fail
test_reserved_label_provides_rejected
```

## Integration Tests (CLI)
```rust
// tests/integration/label_tests.rs
#[test]
fn test_label_add_basic() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue for labeling");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "urgent"])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Added label"));
    
    // Verify label was added
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("urgent"));
}

#[test]
fn test_label_add_idempotent() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "test"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Adding again should succeed (idempotent)
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "test"])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("exists").or(predicate::str::contains("already")));
}

#[test]
fn test_label_add_multiple_issues() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id1, &id2, "shared-label"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Verify both have the label
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list", &id1])
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("shared-label"));
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list", &id2])
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("shared-label"));
}

#[test]
fn test_label_remove_basic() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "to-remove"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "remove", &id, "to-remove"])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Removed"));
    
    // Verify label was removed
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list", &id, "--json"])
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("to-remove").not());
}

#[test]
fn test_label_remove_idempotent() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue");
    
    // Remove label that doesnt exist should succeed
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "remove", &id, "nonexistent"])
        .assert()
        .success();
}

#[test]
fn test_label_list_issue() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue with labels");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "bug"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "urgent"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    let labels = json.as_array().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(labels.len(), 2);
    assert!(labels.contains(&json!("bug")));
    assert!(labels.contains(&json!("urgent")));
}

#[test]
fn test_label_list_all() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id1, "shared"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id2, "shared"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id1, "unique"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list-all", "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    let labels = json.as_array().unwrap();
    
    // Find shared label - should have count 2
    let shared = labels.iter().find(|l| l["label"] == "shared").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(shared["count"], 2);
    
    // Find unique label - should have count 1
    let unique = labels.iter().find(|l| l["label"] == "unique").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(unique["count"], 1);
}

#[test]
fn test_label_case_sensitive() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Case test");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "Bug"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "bug"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "BUG"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    let labels = json.as_array().unwrap();
    // All three should exist (case-sensitive)
    assert_eq!(labels.len(), 3);
}

#[test]
fn test_label_invalid_chars_fail() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Invalid label test");
    
    // Labels with spaces should fail
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "invalid label"])
        .assert()
        .failure()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("Invalid label"));
    
    // Labels with special chars should fail
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "label@special"])
        .assert()
        .failure();
}

#[test]
fn test_label_valid_chars() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Valid chars test");
    
    // Alphanumeric should work
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "label123"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Dash should work
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "high-priority"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Underscore should work
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "needs_review"])
        .assert()
        .success();
}

#[test]
fn test_label_reserved_prefix() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Reserved label test");
    
    // provides: prefix should be rejected/warned
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "provides:auth"])
        .assert()
        .failure()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("reserved"));
}

#[test]
fn test_label_json_output_add() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "JSON output test");
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id, "test-label", "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json.is_array());
    let result = &json[0];
    assert_eq!(result["status"], "added");
    assert!(result["issue_id"].is_string());
    assert_eq!(result["label"], "test-label");
}

#[test]
fn test_label_nonexistent_issue() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", "bd-nonexistent", "label"])
        .assert()
        .failure()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("not found"));
}

#[test]
fn test_label_rename() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id1, "old-name"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "add", &id2, "old-name"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "rename", "old-name", "new-name"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Verify old name gone
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["label", "list-all", "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(!json.as_array().unwrap().iter().any(|l| l["label"] == "old-name"));
    
    // Verify new name exists with count 2
    let new_label = json.as_array().unwrap().iter().find(|l| l["label"] == "new-name").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(new_label["count"], 2);
}
```

}

 {"id":"beads_rust-d28m","title":"Conformance: CRUD Command Expansion (init, create, list, show, update, delete, close, reopen)","description":"# Conformance: CRUD Command Expansion

## Background
CRUD commands are the bread-and-butter of br. While some have basic conformance tests, they need comprehensive edge case coverage. These commands handle the core issue lifecycle.

## Current Coverage Analysis
| Command | Current Tests | Gaps |
|---------|---------------|------|
| init | 1 | Re-init, already initialized, permissions |
| create | 2 | All flags, very long titles, special chars, duplicates |
| list | 3 | All filters (status, priority, assignee, label, type), pagination, sorting |
| show | 1 | Non-existent ID, partial ID matching, deleted issues |
| update | 1 | All fields, partial updates, concurrent updates |
| delete | 1 | Already deleted, with dependencies, tombstone behavior |
| close | 1 | Already closed, with reason, blocked issue |
| reopen | 1 | Never closed, tombstone, preserves fields |

## New Tests to Add

### delete (5 new tests)

### close (6 new tests)

### reopen (5 new tests)

 {"id":"beads_rust-dc4","title":"Implement --robot-triage flag for ranked actionable items","description":"# --robot-triage Flag Implementation

## Purpose
Provide AI coding agents with a ranked list of actionable items, including scores, reasons, and unblock information to enable intelligent work selection.

## Technical Requirements

### Output Format
```json
{
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "id": "beads_rust-xyz",
      "title": "Implement search command",
      "priority": 1,
      "score": 0.95,
      "reasons": [
        "High priority (P1)",
        "Unblocks 3 other issues",
        "No dependencies",
        "Estimated small scope"
      ],
      "claim_command": "br update beads_rust-xyz --status in_progress",
      "unblocks": ["beads_rust-abc", "beads_rust-def"]
    }
  ],
  "project_health": {
    "total": 150,
    "open": 100,
    "in_progress": 20,
    "blocked": 30,
    "ready": 50
  }
}
```

### Scoring Algorithm
- Priority weight: P0=1.0, P1=0.8, P2=0.6, P3=0.4, P4=0.2
- Unblock multiplier: +0.1 per issue unblocked
- Age bonus: older ready issues get slight boost
- Dependency penalty: issues with many deps score lower

### Implementation
```rust
fn calculate_triage_score(issue: \u0026Issue, graph: \u0026DepGraph) -> f64 {
    let priority_weight = match issue.priority {
        0 => 1.0,
        1 => 0.8,
        2 => 0.6,
        3 => 0.4,
        _ => 0.2,
    };
    let unblock_bonus = graph.dependents(\u0026issue.id).len() as f64 * 0.1;
    priority_weight + unblock_bonus
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `br --robot-triage` outputs valid JSON
- [ ] Issues are sorted by score descending
- [ ] Reasons explain the ranking
- [ ] claim_command is correct and runnable
- [ ] Project health summary included

## Dependencies
